name: F56 - Create AND remove entire-domain safe sender (full lifecycle)
description: Open Manage Safe Senders screen, tap Add FAB, select Entire Domain type, enter a uniquely-named test domain (winwright-e2e-test.invalid), save, verify the new safe sender appears, then delete it and verify removal. Uses .invalid TLD per RFC 6761 to guarantee no real-world email collision; pattern parser accepts it because validation is structural (regex), not IANA membership lookup.
prerequisites: test_data.domain is NOT pre-existing in safe_senders table

test_data.rationale: Per RFC 6761, .invalid is a reserved TLD guaranteed not to resolve in DNS, so this domain cannot collide with any real safe sender. The unique 'winwright-e2e-test' label avoids collision even if .invalid were on a future blocklist.

steps.comment: Open Settings (header opens account-selection dialog first per Sprint 35 finding)
Pick first account in selection dialog
Open Manage Safe Senders from Settings General tab
Verify FAB present
Tap FAB
Verify ManualRuleCreateScreen in safeSender mode
Verify TLD type is NOT shown for safe senders (per F56 design)
Select Entire Domain type
Focus the input field
Enter unique test domain (RFC 6761 .invalid TLD prevents real-world collision)
Verify pattern preview contains source domain
Use ww_invoke (UIA pattern) since Save Rule may be off-screen
Confirm save
Back at Manage Safe Senders screen
Focus search bar to filter for our test entry
Search for the unique test marker
VERIFY CREATE: the new safe sender appears in filtered list
Open detail dialog to access Delete button
Safe sender detail dialog opened
Tap Delete in the safe sender detail dialog
Wait for confirmation dialog
Confirm deletion
VERIFY DELETE: safe sender is no longer in the filtered list
Clear search filter to restore default view
